GRAPHIC DESIGN 
November 28, 1977 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Gentlemen: 
At the request of the County Administrative Officer 
for Humboldt County, I am submitting the enclosed 
series of letters. 
These letters reflect the cir-
cumstances and events related to a design proposal 
submitted by me to the County of Humboldt. 
It is 
the County Adminstrative Officer's desire that you 
make a ruling on this particular situation before 
the County directs me to proceed in designing the 
State Capitol Building display. 
Your consideration of this matter is prompted by 
the fact that I am an appointed member of the 
Humboldt County Tourism Advisory Board which rec-
ommended to the Board of Supervisors that I be 
considered for this design assignment. 
The rec-
ommendation was based on my professional qualifi-
cations and the fact that I had initially been 
requested by the Supervisors to make recommendat-
ions on our county's deteriorating Capitol display. 
My recommendations were reviewed by the Board of 
Supervisors and the Tourism Development Advisory 
Board. 
The Board of Supervisors then directed the 
County Administrative Officer to request a formal 
proposal from me. 
The proposal to redesign the 
Capitol exhibit was accepted and now we would like 
your board to rule on a possible conflict of interest 
before we enter into a contract. 
If any additional information is needed, please 
contact me. 
We look forward to your ruling on this 
matter. 
Very truly yours, 
Nelson Richardson 
Enclosures 
CC: 
Mr. Ron Holden, C.A.O., Humboldt County 


GRAPHIC DESIGN 
PRESENT A TIO/l./ ART 
ADVERTISING ART 
ARCH. RENDERINGS 
NELSON RICHARDSON 
1920 WILLIAMS ST. 
EUREKA. CA. 95501 
PH. (07) 445~2481 
September 2, 1977 
Mr. Ron Holden 
County Administrative Officer 
825 Fifth Street 
Eureka, California 
95501 
Dear Mr. Holden: 
In response to the Board of Supervisors recent direc-
tive, I am pleased to submit a proposal to design and 
construct a new exhibit representing Humboldt County 
in the State Capitol Building. 
I propose to design and construct a colorful three-
dimensional display that will emphasize our County's 
appeal to the tourist. 
The exciting exhibit will 
stand out from other county exhibits and attract the 
vacationer to our area. 
Although dynamic in its for-
mat, the display will require no maintenance which 
has been a problem in the past. 
The content of the 
exhibit will be in keeping with contemporary design 
standards and should serve as an outstanding example 
of Humboldt County's offerings. 
The design sequence would include a preliminary input 
meeting with those interested supervisors. 
Upon con-
cept approval, fabrication would commence. 
The 
exhibit could be installed eight to ten weeks after 
contract approval. 
The estimated cost for design, 
construction, delivery and installation in Sacramento 
would be $1,425.00. 
My professional abilities are reflected by a Masters 
Degree in Art and eight years of graphic communica-
tion experience. 
Many local business recommendations 
can be provided at your request. 
My clients have 
included Winzler & Kelly, Consulting Engineers, 
Simpson Timber Company, Eureka Fisheries and the Ingo-
mar Club. 
I look forward to serving the County in this design 
capacity. 
Very truly yours, 
Nelson Richardson 


August 11, 1977 
Board of Supervisors 
825 Fifth Street 
Eureka, CA 95501 
Supervisors, 
TOURISM DEVELOPMENT ADVISORY BOARD 
COUNTY 
OF 
HUf~BOlDT

Regarding the Humboldt County display in the Capitol Building in Sacramento, 
the members of the Tourism Development Advisory Board feel that such an 
important project as the redesigning of the display should be left to the 
capable and artistic hands of a professional designer. 
After the remodeling of the Capitol Building is complete, there is expected 
to be a definite increase in the volume of tourists passing through and viewing 
our display. 
You vli11 agree that the more dynamic and sophisticated our 
display is, the better able it will be to compete with the displays of the 
larger counties and shine through as being the most worthy of the tourists 
vacations (and dollars~). 
At the August 10 TDAB meeting the taking of bids for the project was carefully 
considered----ho\'Jever, we have a professional designer right in our ovm midst. 
Nelson Richardson has traveled to Sacramento to look at and 
repail~ that very 
di sp lay a short time ago and is wi 11 i ng to devote the time and energy in 
redesigning it. He also.is very familiar with the beauty and attractions of 
humboldt County and knows his resources. 
The JI.dvisory Goard, therefore, respectfully requests t:lat [lelson Richardson, 
be contacted by you and authorized to pursue the redesigning of the display: 
The 
final design to be approved by the Board of Supervisors. 
Sincerely, 
~B,,~ 
Ann Goyer for the Tourism Development Advisory Board

view the displays which have potential bearing on travel decisions.

The county should present

state. 
The displays are constantly compared to each other as a focal point 
within the Capitol 
lex. 
A nevi dynamic presentation of vJhat this 
coun

additional tourist interest, especially 
novJ as the Capitol building p

(Six new displ 
have been installed this year.) 
I suggest that the 
a professional d~sign service to create the best possible

or group displ

DATE; 
TO: 
FROM: 
May 17, 1977 
COUNTY OF HUMBOLDT 
Inter-Office Memo 
Members of the Board of Supervisors 
R. S., Holden, County Administrative Officer 
SUBJECT: Replacement of County's Capitol Exhibit 
Approximately three weeks ago, I received a call from Mr. Nelson 
Richardson who is associated with the County's Tourism Development 
Advisory Board. 
In essence, 'Carl Schillinger had' spoken to Mr. 
Richardson concerning the repair/replacement of the County's exhibit 
at the State Capitol. 
Mr. Richardson is employed by Hinzler and 
Kelly and has an artistic background. 
Mr. Richardson is willing to assist in the prepare of· the exhibit. 
At the time I spoke with him, he was intending to combine the 
replacement of the exhibit with his vacation. 
This is now not likely 
to be a possibility, and it will require a special effort to gain Mr. 
Richardson's assistance in this effort. 
The purpose of this memorandum is merely to inform the Board of this 
contact and to request that you alert me as to when Board members will 
be traveling to Sacramento. 
If sufficient lead time is provided, then 
perhaps it \vill be possible to have Mr. Richardson also journey to 
Sacramento to examine the exhibit and then to do the work necessary to 
prepare a replacement. 
I am, by means of copies of this memorandum, informing Mrs .. Schillinger 
and Mr. Richardson that Mr. Richardson may be requested to journey to 
Sacramen~o at just about any time. 
Mr. Richardson's phone number is 
443-8326. 
Please contact me if you have questions or wish to discuss the contents 
of this memorandum. 
RSH/pjs 
cc:, 
~s. Carol Schillinger 
~r. Nelson Richardson 
R. S. HOLDEN 
County Administrative Officer 
'R:t=G 
MAY 19 
IV D 
WINZLER &. KELLY 
CONSULTING ENGINEERS 


COUNTY ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE 
COUNTY 
OF 
HUMBOLDT 
EUREKA. CALIFORNIA 95501 
PHONE (707) 445 -7266 
Building Manager 
State Capitol Building 
Room Bl12 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Sir: 
June 2, 1977 
Please accept this letter as formal authorization from the 
Humboldt County Board of Supervisors for Mr. Nelson Richardson 
to examine and repair/replace the County's exhibit at the State 
Capitol Building. 
Mr. Dave Zebo was previously authorized to do this work for the 
County, but is. retiring very soon. 
The Board is anxious to have the exhibit reconditioned as soon 
as possible, and Mr. Richardson will probably be making more 
than one trip to Sacramento to accomplish this. 
If the services 
of your office are required to undertake this project, you have 
the Board's sincere appreciation and thanks for your cooperation. 
Sincerely, 
R. S. HOLDEN 
County Administrative Officer 
/lj 


COUNTY ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE 
COUNTY 
OF 
HUMBOLDT 
EUREKA. CALIFORNIA 95501 
PHONE (707) 445-7266 
Mrs. Carol Schillinger 
1460 Fickle Hill Road 
Arcata, California 
95521 
Dear Carol: 
March 30, 1977 
SUBJECT: 
State Capitol Window Display 
During its session of March 15, 1977, the Board received the 
attached Los Angeles County Fair Association letter and referred 
it to the Tourism Development Advisory Board for its recommenda-
tion. 
A bi-product 6f the discussion of this letter were remarks 
concerning the condition of Humboldt County's Window Display at 
the State Capitol. 
In essence, it has fallen into a state of 
disrepair. 
The Board thought that you might be interested in accepting the 
chore to repair the window if the necessary travel and materials 
arrangements can be made. 
My understanding is that special arrange-
ments have to be made with the State Capitol Maintenance Department 
prior to even gaining access to the window. 
Your willingness to take on this chore would be sincerely appre-
ciated by the Board, and I am sure that we can make the necessary 
arrangements in advance so that you may make necessary personal 
arrangements to journey to Sacramento. 
The County would, of 
course, cover the necessary expenses. 
You willingness to consider this request is greatly appreciated. 
Please contact me if you desire further information. 
RSH/pjs 
Attachment 
Sincerely, 
R. S. HOLDEN 
County Administrative Officer

Chairman, Tourism Development Advisory Board

sections.conclusion: Accordingly, we conclude that you are not a "public official" within the meaning of Government Code Section 87100 by virtue of your membership on the Tourism Development Advisory Board. Accordingly, the strictures of the Political Reform Act do not prohibit your participation in that capacity.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Nelson Richardson, as an appointed member of the Humboldt County Tourism Development Advisory Board that recommended him for a design contract, face a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when entering into a contract with the County to redesign the State Capitol Building display?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that Richardson is not a public official under Government Code Section 87100 because the Tourism Development Advisory Board is not a decision-making body and Richardson is not an officer, employee, or consultant of the County by virtue of his board membership. However, the FPPC noted that Government Code Section 1090 prohibiting certain contracts where financial interests exist may apply, which is outside FPPC's interpretive authority.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter from Richardson dated November 28, 1977, and FPPC response letter from Kenneth W. Goshorn dated December 30, 1977. Supporting documents include initial proposal and advisory board recommendation letters. Some text is heavily degraded/OCR corrupted, affecting full clarity of certain passages.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Nelson Richardson, as an appointed member of the Humboldt County Tourism Development Advisory Board that recommended him for a design contract, face a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when entering into a contract with the County to redesign the State Capitol Building display?

CONCLUSION: Accordingly, we conclude that you are not a "public official" within the meaning of Government Code Section 87100 by virtue of your membership on the Tourism Development Advisory Board. Accordingly, the strictures of the Political Reform Act do not prohibit your participation in that capacity.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to an inquiry regarding whether Nelson Richardson, a graphic designer and member of Humboldt County's Tourism Development Advisory Board, faces a conflict of interest in accepting a design contract from the County for the State Capitol Building display that the Advisory Board recommended to him. The FPPC concluded Richardson is not subject to Political Reform Act restrictions as he is not a public official, though other statutory prohibitions may apply.
